channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: The spiky cactus fruit giving Indian farmers a cash boost
item.description: In 2020 he abandoned his career in finance, sold all his shares and gold and invested the money in the family farm, in the southern Indian state of Karnataka.

item.title: World Cup playoffs: Poland fight back to sink Albania, Kosovo win thriller in Slovakia
item.description: Robert Lewandowski helped Poland come from behind to scrape past Albania 2-1 after Arber Hoxha gave the visitors the lead in Warsaw.

item.title: The 'crown jewels' of the Iranian nuclear program and why the US wants them
item.description: Hidden in the mountains of Iran, there is a stockpile of canisters that could determine the future of the war.

item.title: Cape York stations hit by Cyclone Narelle call for army's help to clean up
item.description: While far north Queensland was largely spared from major damage, some property owners were smashed by Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

item.title: Max Verstappen kicks Guardian F1 reporter out of Japanese Grand Prix press conference
item.description: Four-time world champion Max Verstappen has kicked a British journalist out of a Formula 1 press conference ahead of this weekend's Japanese Grand Prix.

item.title: Sinner continues smooth Miami progress with win over Tiafoe as rivals fall
item.description: The past nine days at the Miami Open have not been kind to most of the world’s best male tennis players.

item.title: Sarah Ferguson stripped of Freedom of City of York title
item.description: Sarah Ferguson has had her honorary Freedom of the City of York stripped, in a further fall from grace for the former Duchess of York.

item.title: Intel Core Ultra 270K and 250K Plus review: Conditionally great CPUs
item.description: Great performance for the price, if you ignore the price of RAM, SSDs, and GPUs.

item.title: OpenAI “indefinitely” shelves plans for erotic ChatGPT
item.description: Some staff reportedly questioned how sexy ChatGPT benefits humanity.

item.title: Tom Brady, 48, says he explored return to NFL but league ‘don’t like that idea very much’
item.description: Tom Brady says he explored the idea of making a return to the NFL as a player but the league “don’t like that idea very much”.

item.title: Paul McCartney announces 18th solo album, The Boys of Dungeon Lane, promising introspection and revelation
item.description: Paul McCartney has announced his 18th solo album, The Boys of Dungeon Lane – its title a reference to the route from Liverpool to the Speke shoreline, the area where the former Beatle spent his young childhood.

item.title: ‘The internet has seen me at my best and my worst’: meet Jojo, Australia’s ASMR superstar
item.description: It would be crass for Jonah Singer to reveal what he makes from being a YouTube content creator, but he will show Guardian Australia his new $100,000 mic.

item.title: Far-fetched to think top aide faked phone theft ahead of request for Mandelson texts, says PM
item.description: The prime minister has said it is "a little bit far-fetched" to believe his former chief-of-staff Morgan McSweeney could have faked the theft of his phone.

item.title: British designer Christopher Kane to take over as creative director at Mulberry
item.description: British fashion designer Christopher Kane has been named as the new creative director of Mulberry.

item.title: Dodging the ‘wrinkle wagon’: why a Brazilian film about ageing is inspiring older women
item.description: When 80-year-old Gilda Olinto was given a prize at work recently, she felt as if she was being told “nothing more is expected of you”.

item.title: Country star Ty Herndon: ‘The drugs could be forgiven. Being gay definitely could not’
item.description: On 14 June 1995, Nashville singer Ty Herndon was riding high with a No 1 single on the country charts and an album hurtling towards gold status when he was arrested on charges that could have killed his career in an instant.

item.title: Returning ospreys avoid last season's love drama
item.description: A pair of ospreys appear to have avoided last year's love triangle drama after both returned to their nest.

item.title: Bruno Fernandes is the true custodian of Manchester United in the age of Ratcliffe | Jonathan Liew
item.description: The video of Bruno Fernandes kicking in the door is very good, if you haven’t already seen it.

item.title: ‘It’s like having a friend everywhere you travel’: after 12 home exchanges, I’ll never book a hotel again
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: AFL braces for increase in positive tests under ‘stronger’ new illicit drugs policy
item.description: The AFL is preparing for an increase in players testing positive to drugs such as cocaine and MDMA due to more effective testing under a new illicit substances policy, even if the public will never find out.

item.title: Popovic wants World Cup contenders to play with freedom in final home matches
item.description: The Socceroos will play two 'farewell' matches against Cameroon and Curaçao in Sydney and Melbourne over the coming days.

item.title: ‘You go in bald and walk out with the best hair you’ve ever had’: the remarkable return of the toupee
item.description: Hair transplants have never been more widespread or more affordable.

item.title: What's a 'super' El Niño, and is Australia facing one?
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: 'My left foot was in his mouth': Fisher's lucky escape from 3.5m shark
item.description: Amateur fisher Luke Kuhn says he is lucky to have escaped an encounter with a great white shark that bit his swim fin and dragged him along the ocean floor.

item.title: The Matildas’ coach wants the side to face the world’s best – so why are they playing Malawi next? | Samantha Lewis
item.description: Did someone forget to include Matildas head coach Joe Montemurro on the emails?.

item.title: ‘It’s something bling’: Gout Gout ready to sparkle as he enters new phase of sprint career
item.description: Dazzling in his sponsor’s tracksuit, and sparkling with an earring and two glimmering chains, sprint sensation Gout Gout told his first press conference in Melbourne his confidence is growing.

item.title: Andrew Hastie open to 25% tax on gas profits and says multinationals have ‘had a really good run’ on Australian wealth
item.description: Liberal frontbencher Andrew Hastie says he is open to a new 25% tax on soaring gas profits as part of a Scandinavian-style sovereign wealth fund to strengthen the federal budget amid the global energy crisis.

item.title: Camilla's love of books explored in BBC documentary
item.description: Queen Camilla is to appear in a BBC documentary about the life-changing power of books.

item.title: The homeless teenager who became a successful advertising boss
item.description: When Greg Daily found himself homeless as a teenager, becoming a successful entrepreneur seemed an impossibility.

item.title: British suppliers to be prioritised for contracts in sectors vital to national security
item.description: British suppliers will be prioritised for public contracts in shipbuilding, steel, AI and energy infrastructure under new guidance marking them out as sectors vital to national security.

item.title: Hannah Gadsby, Aaron Chen and more share their picks of the comedy festival
item.description: Luke Heggie was 37 when he did his first show at Melbourne International Comedy Festival in 2012.

item.title: Health, education, tax and more - Has the SNP delivered on its promises at Holyrood?
item.description: Holyrood has broken up for May's election, and parties will spend the next six weeks debating their policies for the future of Scotland.

item.title: Harry Maguire: ‘I really like Ruben, he’s got great ideas. They just didn’t work at Manchester United’
item.description: Harry Maguire was injured again and Manchester United were stuck in a loop under Ruben Amorim, offering their own take on the definition of insanity.

item.title: Brisbane capitalises on 'less sexy' business events to the tune of millions
item.description: Brisbane is pitching to attract more major business conventions and conferences in the lead up to the 2032 Olympics.

item.title: Bay the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents in Victoria
item.description: Two new reports have listed parts of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula that are likely to be affected by inundation and erosion associated with climate change.

item.title: Manchester United v Bayern Munich: Women’s Champion League quarter-final, first leg – live
item.description: 50 min: Turner concedes a free-kick on halfway.

item.title: Who wants what and why from US-Iran peace talks?
item.description: When one side, the US, says there are ongoing, productive negotiations to end the war and the other side, Iran, says "no, there are not", then who to believe?.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands is rich and driven to win. But the stakes of suing ARN are high
item.description: There's an $88 million question currently doing the rounds of legal and media circles, following the catastrophic falling out between radio shock jocks Kyle and Jackie O and their former radio network ARN.

item.title: Pretty Lethal review – Amazon’s ballerina action thriller puts on a decent enough show
item.description: Last year’s Ballerina, or as Lionsgate’s marketing team would prefer us to say From the World of John Wick: Ballerina, failed as both proof that the Keanu Reeves-led franchise could support expansion and that “ballet action thriller” could be a worthy new genre.

item.title: I thought my cuckoo clock was amazing, but it’s got nothing on my statue of Bert the cheery chef | Adrian Chiles
item.description: I came across something that makes me happy every day.

item.title: Dogs became man's best friend far earlier than thought, scientists find
item.description: A fragment of a jawbone found deep underground in a cave in Somerset has rewritten the story of when and how dogs became our best friends.

item.title: Can Bluey save classical music? Cartoon puppy’s all-encompassing soundtrack plays Bach and Beethoven to billions
item.description: Classical music’s continued battle for relevance and impact continues to find new nadirs, from tired experiments with formats to bathetic look-at-me clickbait.

item.title: As a furniture removalist I learned all mattresses are stained, and that’s fine
item.description: My “wilderness years” began when I lost a job I loved hosting a daily radio show in Melbourne, then spent a decade slowly rebuilding my broadcasting career.

item.title: When my best friend died, I couldn’t bear to delete her phone contact. Here’s why I never will
item.description: A couple of weeks after my oldest friend died, my phone rang and her name lit up the screen.

item.title: Arundhati Roy and Lyse Doucet lead ‘exceptional’ Women’s prize for nonfiction shortlist
item.description: Arundhati Roy, Lyse Doucet and Judith Mackrell are among the writers shortlisted for this year’s Women’s prize for nonfiction.

item.title: Denmark braces for lengthy and challenging coalition talks
item.description: Denmark is braced for lengthy and challenging coalition talks after neither Mette Frederiksen’s leftwing bloc nor the rightwing parties managed to get a majority in Tuesday’s election.

item.title: Yep, a mom's COVID shot during pregnancy protects her baby, a large study finds
item.description: Babies under 6 months old still have one of the highest rates of hospitalization from COVID-19 infections compared to other age groups, but no COVID vaccine is available for these infants.

item.title: Fresh start: Hetty Lui McKinnon’s recipes to celebrate spring
item.description: Vegetables are in my blood.

item.title: 'Fearless' Mel Schilling remembered by Married at First Sight co-star
item.description: Mel Schilling's Married At First Sight UK co-star Paul C Brunson has shared an emotional tribute to his "fearless", "tough" and "deeply loved" late friend and colleague.

item.title: The Celtics’ orca-loving Joe Mazzulla is an NBA oddball. He’s also a masterful coach
item.description: The Boston Celtics’ head coach, Joe Mazzulla, is a very odd man.

item.title: Sali Hughes on beauty: finally, a dry skin remedy with a touch of elegance
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: So long, farewell: Saying goodbye to Audi's best car, the 2026 RS6 Avant
item.description: With production now finished, we take one last ride in our favorite station wagon.

item.title: Stocks rise and oil dips on hopes of 15-point Iran peace plan
item.description: The price of oil has dipped and Asian stock markets moved higher after reports that Donald Trump has sent a 15-point framework for peace to Iran, amid hopes of a ceasefire in the Middle East.

item.title: Are you sure you know what 'gaslighting' is?
item.description: You're not imagining it.

item.title: Mette Frederiksen keen to remain as Denmark’s PM despite losing ground in election – Europe live
item.description: Good morning from Copenhagen, where there is no left or right majority and the Moderates’ Lars Løkke Rasmussen is expected to hold the key to coalition discussions.

item.title: Perfect homework, blank stares: Why colleges are turning to oral exams to combat AI
item.description: The assignment involves no laptop, no chatbot and no technology of any kind.

item.title: Playful lynx snatches top prize in photo competition
item.description: A young Iberian lynx caught mid play has won the Wildlife Photographer of the Year People's Choice Award 2026.

item.title: 'Liverpool music trail will inspire new musicians'
item.description: A new tourist trail celebrating Liverpool's rich musical heritage has been designed to inspire a new generation of creative talent.

item.title: Bait review – Riz Ahmed’s comedy is petty, narcissistic … and excellent
item.description: Part autobiography, part industry satire, this hilarious show boasts dazzling dialogue and ace cameos.

item.title: Bridgerton’s fifth season will focus on queer love story between Francesca Bridgerton and Michaela Stirling
item.description: Dearest gentle reader … Netflix has announced that Bridgerton’s upcoming fifth season will focus on a gay lead storyline in a first for the hit romantic period drama.

item.title: ‘With solar and batteries so popular, will all Australians generate their own power?’ - Your climate questions answered
item.description: The US-Israel war on Iran has caused a surge in fossil fuel prices – again.

item.title: Australia politics live: NSW to compel oil companies to explain where extra petrol being sent; Victoria announces three hours of free power a day
item.description: The NSW government will compel major fuel companies to explain how they will distribute extra fuel released from federal reserves in the first use of emergency powers in the state to address shortages.

item.title: More frequent ejaculations may boost men’s fertility, research suggests
item.description: Encouraging men to have more frequent ejaculations may boost their fertility, according to researchers who found that sperm deteriorates over time as it remains in the body.

item.title: Married at First Sight expert remembered as 'amazing' and 'radiant'
item.description: Mel Schilling emerged as one of Australia's most recognisable relationship experts on Australian television.

item.title: How Melbourne ended up with the biggest comedy festival in the world
item.description: Paul McDermott doesn't remember a lot from the first Melbourne International Comedy Festival (MICF) 40 years ago, where he performed as part of the now-legendary musical comedy group, the Doug Anthony All Stars.

item.title: Like millions of others, I pull my own hair out - we need to talk about trichotillomania
item.description: An online content creator who has a compulsive hair-pulling disorder says others with the condition "shouldn't be ashamed" or "feel embarrassed".

item.title: ‘I will never walk alone’: Mohamed Salah to leave Liverpool on free transfer at end of season
item.description: Mohamed Salah has announced he will leave Liverpool at the end of the season, bringing to a close one of the greatest careers in the club’s storied history.

item.title: Arsenal v Chelsea: Women’s Champions League quarter-final, first leg – live
item.description: 49 min: Arsenal have a corner.

item.title: 'The day has come': Liverpool great announces he is leaving EPL giant
item.description: Mo Salah has announced he is leaving EPL giants Liverpool despite having a year left on his contract.

item.title: NDIS participants work more and feel better after accessing the scheme
item.description: People who access the NDIS fare better in the workforce and feel healthier within four years of entering the scheme, a new report finds.

item.title: Gary is part of the super-rich. He believes wealthy Australians should be paying more tax
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Vivid dreams can make you feel like you've had a deep sleep: study
item.description: Vivid and immersive dreams can create the perception of deeper sleep, new research suggests.

item.title: Everyday life in Asia being upended by effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz after the US and Israel began their war with Iran in late February has sent shockwaves across the globe.

item.title: Bridgerton puts female romance at heart of season five
item.description: We've barely recovered from season four of Bridgerton, but now details of series five have been announced - with a new romance to set hearts aflutter.

item.title: Shoplifting, sex shows and sheepdog-breeding: great artists and the side-hustles they did to get by
item.description: For as long as young people have dreamed of careers in the arts – as novelists, painters, poets, musicians and other species – they have had to measure their dreams against their economic circumstances.

item.title: Voting under way in Denmark as Greenland PM says election is most important in territory’s history – Europe live
item.description: Denmark election: far right has slowed under Frederiksen – but at what cost?.

item.title: Reeves plans energy bill help for those 'who need it most'
item.description: The government plans to help "those who need it most" if energy bills spiral due to the US-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: What’s the best material for a chopping board, and how to avoid mould?
item.description: I saw an influencer advocating for titanium chopping boards.

item.title: Manchester United fan, 76, feeling ‘helpless’ as family seat is given to VIPs
item.description: A Manchester United fan said he feels “helpless and hopeless” after being evicted from the seat his family have held since just after the second world war to make way for £300-a-head VIPs.

item.title: The Breakdown | Fiji and the beautiful south reveal rugby’s soul but northern money talks loudest
item.description: They’re present in every top-flight club in Europe.

item.title: A Canadian goal threat to a versatile Spaniard: three of the best teenagers in women’s football
item.description: Vicky López, Lily Yohannes, Michelle Agyemang — these are all names you may have heard before.

item.title: More North Sea drilling will put UK at mercy of fossil fuel markets, ministers say
item.description: Ministers have said expanding North Sea drilling would put the UK at further risk from volatile fossil fuel markets, amid calls from the Conservatives and some Labour MPs to breach the manifesto pledge of no new oil and gas licences.

item.title: Molly Miller, ‘pretty privilege’ and women’s basketball’s beauty trap
item.description: In March 2025, the Arizona State women’s basketball team were looking for a coach who could end a drought that had seen them go without a NCAA Tournament appearance – or even a winning season – since 2019-20.

item.title: More sightings of the Northern Lights with stunning bright colours
item.description: The Northern Lights were seen in the UK again on Monday night following an increase in solar activity over the last few days.

item.title: Villagers 'proud' of overturning second home crackdown in 'David and Goliath moment'
item.description: A group of villagers who fought to overturn a council's crackdown on second home-ownership say they are "proud" of their "David and Goliath moment".

item.title: Regional charity struggles to rescue more than 100 cats from property
item.description: More than 100 cats have been found at a property at Wodonga on the Victoria–New South Wales border.

item.title: Are you 'panic-buying' or 'playing it safe'?
item.description: If you've topped up your tank at a petrol station recently, did it feel like you were "panic buying"? Or did it feel more like "I'd better buy some, just in case"?.

item.title: Cheaper EVs, easier job access: What's in the EU-Australia free trade deal for you?
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: The global authoritarian right loves Orbán – and that could cost him in Hungary’s elections | Gellert Tamas
item.description: “Viktor Orbán is a true friend, fighter, and WINNER, and has my Complete and Total Endorsement for Re-Election as Prime Minister of Hungary,” Donald Trump wrote on Truth Social last month.

item.title: More than 100 petrol stations run out of petrol across Victoria, energy minister says
item.description: Service stations across Victoria have run out of petrol and diesel, as the war in the Middle East increases demand, the state's energy minister says.

item.title: Meat exporters criticise $10 billion free trade agreement as Australia, EU hail 'win-win'
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: UK universities flock to India - but will they succeed?
item.description: On the shores of Mumbai's Powai lake - a rare pocket of serenity in the bustling metropolis of more than 20 million people - a glitzy new campus of the University of York is getting its final finishing touches.

item.title: 'We treated Natalie like a princess, we'll love her forever'
item.description: It's Sunday 18 December 2022 and Lionel Messi is lifting the World Cup trophy on TV while a delighted Natalie McNally watches on.

item.title: Why is Pauline’s vote soaring? She’s got friends in high places | Fiona Katauskas
item.description: The One Nation leader is not as grounded as you thinkSee more of Fiona Katauskas’s cartoons here Continue reading.

item.title: BOM says more rain causing 'significant river level rises' in soaked NT
item.description: BOM has a number of active weather warnings in the Northern Territory, including the potential for moderate and major flooding in several rivers.

item.title: Victoria Mboko and Mirra Andreeva lead new generation of friendly rivals
item.description: Victoria Mboko and Mirra Andreeva, the two highest-ranked teenagers in the world, prepared for their marquee Miami Open fourth-round match in an unusual manner.

item.title: Young musicians bring the beauty of music and maths to life
item.description: Old stereotypes often paint creative pursuits like music as worlds apart from subjects such as maths and science.

item.title: Senate to vote on DHS chief, with TSA funding and SAVE act tied up in Congress
item.description: The Senate votes Monday on the nomination of Sen.

item.title: First female archbishop of Canterbury highlights progress and ongoing challenges
item.description: On a bright morning in Hermosa Beach, Calif., sunlight enters St.

item.title: ‘I enjoy when things get tough’: Alessia Russo on Arsenal’s trophy quest, family pride and staying focused
item.description: Alessia Russo is happy and it shows.

item.title: Live: Albanese, EU boss give green light to free trade deal
item.description: After almost a decade of negotiations, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has confirmed Australia has struck a free trade agreement with the European Union.

item.title: Australia and European Union to sign free trade agreement decades in the making
item.description: Australia and the European Union look set to finally sign off on a long-awaited free trade pact after almost a decade of negotiations.

item.title: Aged care algorithm 'like Russian roulette'
item.description: The algorithm to determine a person's eligibility for in-home care came into effect in November.

item.title: Viktor Orbán celebrated by Europe’s far right before Hungary election
item.description: Marine Le Pen has called Viktor Orbán “an exceptional leader” and Geert Wilders hailed “a lion on a continent led by sheep” as Europe’s far-right figureheads rallied round Hungary’s prime minister before an election that polls suggest he may lose.

item.title: WNBA players unanimously approve a new 7-year collective bargaining agreement through 2032
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — WNBA players unanimously approved the new collective bargaining agreement on Monday with more than 90% participating in voting over the weekend.

item.title: Blades of glory (or not): what makes a chef’s knife truly great?
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: AI will tell you Australia’s fuel reserve is in the US. But is that even true?
item.description: If you Google where Australia’s emergency fuel reserves are held, the search engine’s AI overview will tell you that a “significant portion” of it is stored overseas in the US.

item.title: Cecily Brown: ‘I was too shy to talk to all these super cool kids like Sarah Lucas and Damien Hirst’
item.description: You would think, then, that she’d be returning triumphant.

item.title: ‘There’s biological treasure here’: Chile’s endemic seals gain protection with new marine park
item.description: Six decades ago, pioneering oceanographer and conservationist Sylvia Earle made a bittersweet discovery while diving off Chile’s oceanic islands with the US National Science Foundation vessel, the Anton Bruun.

item.title: A unique NASA satellite is falling out of orbit—this team is trying to rescue it
item.description: Katalyst Space Technologies must launch the Swift rescue mission by this summer.

item.title: Welcome to the United States of Mancunia
item.description: A new wave of hyper-regional hoagies, subs and pizzas are taking over Manchester’s food scene.

item.title: Pro-EU liberals see narrow win in Slovenia as key Italian referendum enters final hours – Europe live
item.description: Slovenia’s incumbent liberal prime minister Robert Golob claimed victory in parliamentary elections on Sunday, as results put his party just slightly ahead of the populist conservatives led by Trump-ally Janez Janša.

item.title: ‘A big smile on my face’: why Everybody Wants Some!! is my feelgood movie
item.description: If any film can make me feel like a teenager again, it’s Everybody Wants Some!!.

item.title: With more older drivers on the road, states try to balance safety and mobility
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis.

item.title: What does a 'GLP-1 Friendly' diet look like? We asked nutritionists
item.description: Some food companies, including Nestle and Conagra, are responding to the popularity of obesity and diabetes drugs by branding some products "GLP-1 Friendly." The labels target a growing number of consumers taking medicines such as Zepbound and Wegovy who may be looking for healthier, ready-made meals that minimize side-effects from the drugs.

item.title: Ukrainians are growing more pessimistic about the prospects for peace, survey says
item.description: As the Iran war delays peace talks for Ukraine — and global supplies of air defense missiles — there are signs that fewer Ukrainians are willing to endure Russia's war for as long as necessary.

item.title: Hull FC turn tide and tame Leeds before era-defining moment for Super League
item.description: It may seem slightly churlish to suggest the pressure is rising in the lower reaches of Super League when the clocks haven’t even gone forward yet and the season is still in its early stages.

item.title: Raye: This Music May Contain Hope review – a wildly ambitious epic of unbridled self-expression
item.description: This story seems telling in light of This Music May Contain Hope, an album that very much suggests an artist determined to go her own way.

item.title: ‘I’m a big bear. I lumber’: showbiz superstar Richard Kind on delivering performances you can see from space
item.description: The first time Kind starred in Nazi-spoof The Producers, he lost 30lb.

item.title: Jane Fonda’s life goes under the microscope: best podcasts of the week
item.description: Pop culture icons and music legends are explored in a new show that has access to the BBC’s vast archive.

item.title: Primary school with just one pupil could be closed for good
item.description: A small school in the Scottish Borders where pupil numbers fell to just one could be closed for good.

item.title: Starwatch: Look west to see the moon blot out Leo’s brightest star
item.description: There is something unexpectedly thrilling about watching one celestial object pass in front of another, an eclipse being the obvious example.

item.title: Flock and awe: inside the big changes at Henry Moore’s glorious sheep-filled Hoglands home
item.description: In September 1940, Henry Moore and his wife, Irina, left London to escape wartime bombing, ending up in the bucolic hamlet of Perry Green, where Hertfordshire meets Essex.

item.title: Have an easier morning thanks to these make-ahead breakfast recipes
item.description: The weekday morning routine can be difficult to perfect, but breakfast doesn't need to be a rushed slice of toast.

item.title: How an island became ferret free - thanks, in part, to Woody the wonderdog
item.description: In a world-first, Rathlin Island, off the coast of County Antrim, has eradicated ferrets.

item.title: Jess Hull says 'my time is coming' after another world championship medal
item.description: Jess Hull took women's silver and Adam Spencer nabbed men's bronze in the 1,500m at the World Athletics Indoor Championships.

item.title: Our son loved being outdoors - now ME means he can't walk or talk
item.description: It is heart-breaking for Myfanwy and Charlie Sleep to watch their once active 28-year-old son lying in bed every day, unable to walk or talk.

item.title: How a 15-year-old boy became a surprise Melbourne Cup winner
item.description: In 1948, country Victoria's Ray Neville stunned the nation, becoming one of the youngest jockeys to win the Melbourne Cup.

item.title: Prince William keen to build 'strong and meaningful' bond with Church
item.description: The Prince of Wales is "keen to build a strong and meaningful bond" with the Church of England, a royal aide has said, ahead of the future monarch attending the new Archbishop of Canterbury's official installation this week.

item.title: US high school student Cooper Lutkenhaus wins 800m to become youngest ever indoor world champion
item.description: US teenager Cooper Lutkenhaus made history on Sunday when he won gold in the 800m to becomes the youngest ever champion at the world indoor athletics championship.

item.title: Barking dogs to shared fences: How to resolve issues with your neighbour
item.description: What often starts as an ordinary issue with a neighbour, like a barking dog or overhanging tree, can snowball into conflict when not handled well.

item.title: Manchester City win Carabao Cup after Nico O’Reilly’s double downs lacklustre Arsenal
item.description: There was a time when the old trophy with the three handles felt like the personal property of Pep Guardiola.

item.title: Matildas need a grand plan to stave off end-of-an-era concerns
item.description: The day after the Matildas' loss to Japan in the final of the Women's Asian Cup, they fronted a 'thank you' event in Sydney, attended by a couple of thousand fans.

item.title: Ollie Watkins puts seal on Villa’s win over West Ham after England omission
item.description: While Ollie Watkins gave the most articulate response to his omission from the England squad with the second-half goal that consolidated Aston Villa’s place in the Premier League’s top four, the most rousing noise around Villa Park arrived when Youri Tielemans replaced John McGinn a minute earlier.

item.title: The Matildas’ near misses sting but their Asian Cup final suggests this great team are not done
item.description: Two steps to the left.

item.title: The kindness of strangers: My kids sent me a birthday fax from far away – a modest man made sure I received it
item.description: In the 1990s I travelled extensively for work, leaving behind my young family.

item.title: Starmer adviser urges ministers to look at profits cap for energy and petrol firms
item.description: The government’s top cost of living adviser has called on ministers to explore a temporary cap on the profits of energy and petrol companies to prevent them from cashing in excessively on the war in the Middle East.

item.title: How to make the perfect cheese khachapuri – recipe | Felicity Cloake's How to make the perfect …
item.description: Tuskadze goes on to explain in her book Supra that there are “as many variations … as there are families in Georgia” – the boat-shaped, open adjaruili that Polina Chesnakova notes has “taken the internet by storm”, the Ossetian mashed potato variety and the Gurian take with hard-boiled eggs and a “supremely fluffy, slightly oniony, soufflé-like cheese filling”, which inspires Caroline Eden to share with readers of her book Green Mountains the glorious Georgian word shemomechama, “which loosely translates as, ‘I accidentally ate the whole thing’”.

item.title: An orthopedic surgeon explains the hand injury that has many MLB players on the bench
item.description: Baseball hitters are on a quest for power.

item.title: This local official thinks he may have the road map for Democrats to win big in 2026
item.description: ALLENTOWN, Pa.

item.title: ‘I want my career, my children and a free supple life’: Sylvia Plath’s radical reinvention
item.description: In February 1962, Sylvia Plath dropped in on her neighbour in Devon, Rose Key, with “a plate of absolutely indigestible Black Walnut-flavored cupcakes”.

item.title: Goggia wins to lock up World Cup super-G title. Aicher further closes gap on overall leader Shiffrin
item.description: KVITFJELL, Norway (AP) — Sofia Goggia won her third super-G of the season at the World Cup finals on Sunday to secure the discipline title, while Emma Aicher further closed the gap to overall leader Mikaela Shiffrin.

item.title: Welcome to allergy season. Here’s how to protect yourself
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Allergy season can be miserable for tens of millions of Americans when trees, grass, and other pollens cause runny noses, itchy eyes, coughing and sneezing.

item.title: Cooking with Angela Hartnett: ‘I love food, but I don’t need to talk about it 24/7’
item.description: Angela Hartnett’s home kitchen isn’t a place you could recreate, however much Le Creuset you bought.

item.title: How tacking centre left will help Labour win the next election | Andy Beckett
item.description: Led by a probably doomed prime minister, presiding over a struggling economy, exposed by an ongoing scandal, besieged by populist insurgents to its right and left, ambushed by a war that will bring higher inflation and public debt, and predicted to win just 75 seats at the next general election, according to the website Electoral Calculus, Labour is in an unprecedented crisis.

item.title: ‘We can create hype’: H&M’s UK boss on its commitment to the high street
item.description: Almost exactly 50 years after H&M opened its first British store, the doors on its newest, in Brighton, swung open this week and the Swedish fashion company’s UK boss is determined to keep investing in the nation’s high streets despite its struggles.

item.title: Slop it like it’s hot: the rise of build-your-own takeaway salad bowls
item.description: How did this pick-n-mix salad-and-protein sludge become a typical working lunch?.

item.title: Scrambling, walking and swimming in splendid isolation: 75 years of the UK’s national parks
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Breaking: One Nation wins first seat in SA Parliament's Lower House, ABC projects
item.description: Pauline Hanson's One Nation has won its first ever Lower House seat at an election outside of Queensland, the ABC projects.

item.title: It’s always me who makes the effort to see my friends. Don’t they value me? | Ask Annalisa Barbieri
item.description: My friends seem genuinely happy to see me when we’re together and usually accept when I suggest meeting up.

item.title: We are living in a period of political anti-intellectualism. But in pop culture, clever is the new cool
item.description: Put down your negroni, hang up your Prada handbag and pick up a paperback.

item.title: Family-friendly TV drama Crookhaven hopes to be latest hit book adaptation
item.description: There's nothing hotter in the world of TV right now than adaptations of popular books, especially those that cater to younger audiences.

item.title: Anyone who goes through IVF 'should be so proud', says Sara Pascoe
item.description: Sara Pascoe has said anyone who tries to conceive through IVF "should be so proud of themselves", explaining that it is "such a big thing to put your body through".

item.title: ‘So awesome’: BTS fans on K-pop’s biggest comeback
item.description: The Zubillaga family had come from Veracruz, Mexico.

item.title: Jess Hull and Kurtis Marschall win bronze as Australia eye more medals on final day at world indoors
item.description: Jess Hull and Kurtis Marschall have won bronze medals at the world athletics indoor championships on another successful day for the Australian team in Poland.

item.title: Is your jerry can safe and legal?
item.description: Despite repeated calls from authorities not to stockpile fuel, warning it will only push prices even higher as demand outpaces supply, some Australians are continuing to fill jerry cans and store petrol at home.

item.title: Hull, Marschall win bronze at World Athletics Indoor Championships
item.description: Jessica Hull won bronze in the women's 3,000 metres at the World Athletics Indoor Championships.

item.title: Three sailors remain missing near Iran. Their families are hoping for a 'miracle'
item.description: Three sailors remain missing after a Thai merchant ship was bombed by Iran as it tried to escape the war zone via the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Superannuation should be used for aged care, not inherited by next generation, aged care CEO says
item.description: Labor should more actively encourage wealthier Australians to spend more of their superannuation on their own care, an industry leader says, to help free up capacity in the struggling system to protect elderly people without means.

item.title: I lost my love of cooking after 12 years as a chef. Moving to a pig farm restored it
item.description: I was a keen-bean 15-year-old when I got my first job in a commercial kitchen in Canberra, raised on a diet of Jamie and Nigella and bursting with a passion for food.

item.title: The Melbourne man who loves libraries so much he created his own – and it’s so huge he needs two homes to house it
item.description: The last time he counted, Prof Wallace Kirsop and his wife, Joan, had about 20,000 books.

item.title: Iraqi Kurds mark Nowruz, celebrating light over darkness
item.description: AKRE, Kurdistan Region of Iraq — For many people, the vernal equinox — which marked the beginning of spring Friday — is just another date on the calendar.

item.title: Fans elated as BTS play first gig together in nearly four years
item.description: Fans have poured into the historic heart of South Korea's capital for what may well be the year's biggest event: the return of the world's most successful band in recent years, BTS.

item.title: ‘I’m going to leave you some landmines’: Pauline Hanson sends SA premier a message amid jubilant supporters
item.description: “Today is not about Pauline Hanson,” Pauline Hanson told the crowd, as she took the microphone to chants of “Pauline! Pauline!”.

item.title: SA state election 2026: Labor secures victory as results show One Nation taking huge chunks of Liberal vote
item.description: Peter Malinauskas is on track for a landslide victory in South Australia’s state election, with Labor easily securing a second term and an increased majority in Saturday’s poll, amid a surge in support for One Nation.

item.title: ‘I was relieved I got the shot without my phone ending up in the sea!’: Easelyn Pineda’s best phone picture
item.description: Pineda waded into the water with her phone to get closer, later making adjustments to ensure the forms of the fishers weren’t lost in silhouette.

item.title: Best thing I ever ate? My first In-N-Out burger in LA
item.description: They say you never forget your first time, but for most of us, this doesn’t apply to cheeseburgers.

item.title: Carol Vorderman: ‘Best kiss of my life? There’s a long list … ’
item.description: Born in Bedfordshire, Carol Vorderman, 65, studied engineering at Cambridge University.

item.title: ‘Tastes of salt, smells of coffee’: why Trieste is one of Italy’s best food cities
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Live: Massive crowd as Matildas take on Japan in blockbuster Asian Cup final
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup final is upon us, with a red-hot Japan standing in the way of the Matildas and what could be their first piece of silverware since 2010.

item.title: Comic Relief helps fund free school uniform charity
item.description: As children across the country ditch school uniform for the day to raise money for Comic Relief, one Greater Manchester charity that has helped thousands of families with recycled uniforms will be receiving funding.

item.title: Becoming a train driver in my 40s was the best thing I ever did
item.description: "I absolutely love it, I wish I'd done it years ago," Sharon Paton says, of her decision to change careers to become a train driver in her 40s.

item.title: Labor's landslide win, as Liberals fall and One Nation rises
item.description: Labor has secured victory in South Australia with Peter Malinauskas winning a second term.

item.title: Love Actually? Washington’s current relationship with Britain is more like Contempt Actually | Timothy Garton Ash
item.description: “A friend who bullies us is no longer a friend.

item.title: Rhoda Roberts, the woman behind 'Welcome to Country', dies at 66
item.description: A guiding force in Australia's arts scene and the woman who coined the term "Welcome to Country", Aunty Rhoda Roberts AO has died at the age of 66.

item.title: ‘A fascinating discovery’: research challenges Battle of Hastings narrative
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Dockers much too strong for Dees, Petracca hurt in Suns' win over Tigers
item.description: Fremantle has overpower Melbourne for a 48-point win, thrilling the home crowd as the Dockers got off the mark in 2026.

item.title: Saturday Night Live UK is here - but can it make you laugh?
item.description: Walking into the Saturday Night Live UK studios for the first time, it's clear much time and money has been pumped into helping this show succeed.

item.title: My love of baking helps me deal with arthritis at age of 13
item.description: After being diagnosed with juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) in 2024, she found it became therapeutic way of coping with a condition that many people she knew had never even heard of.

item.title: Maguire red card costs Manchester United as Kroupi rescues Bournemouth draw
item.description: Under Friday night lights, Manchester United stumbled.

item.title: It's not a royal visit, but something else important happened in Tassie this week
item.description: You'd have to be living under a rock if you missed that "our" Danish Queen Mary visited Hobart this week.

item.title: Small but mighty cheers for team Japan at Asian Cup final
item.description: Young Japanese diaspora in Sydney will be showing up loud and proud in support of Japan against Australia's Matildas at the Asian Cup final on Saturday.

item.title: Polls predict easy Labor win in SA, but One Nation will be closely watched
item.description: One Nation isn't shying away from the national significance of today.

item.title: Top US Fema official claims to have teleported to a Waffle House before
item.description: A far-right conspiracy theorist turned high-ranking official at the US Federal Emergency Management Agency (Fema) claims to have once teleported to a Waffle House.

item.title: Starmer most popular PM - in Toby Jug sales
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer may have had a tough year in office, but in one key measure he is top of the shop: the Toby Jug in his likeness outsold all other prime ministers in 2025.

item.title: Why Ryan Gosling's eyes were so important to Project Hail Mary
item.description: Australian cinematographer Grieg Fraser has recreated pretty much every earthly environment you can think of; from the night vision deserts of Zero Dark Thirty, to the comic-book grime of Gotham City in The Batman to queasy sheen of Washington politics in Vice.

item.title: Work from home and drive more slowly to save energy, global body urges
item.description: With energy prices still high due to the conflict in the Gulf, governments around the world are being urged to reduce driving speed limits and encourage more working from home.

item.title: ‘We need to think much bigger’: trade minister calls for greater ambition in UK-EU reset
item.description: It was all smiles and warm handshakes when the two men in charge of renegotiating the UK’s relationship with the EU met in Brussels this week.

item.title: Jehovah’s Witnesses ease policy on transfusions, allowing storage and use of one’s own blood
item.description: Leaders of Jehovah’s Witnesses are modifying their prohibition on receiving blood transfusions on religious grounds, now allowing members to decide whether to allow their own blood to be drawn and stored in advance for such things as a scheduled surgery with a risk of significant blood loss.

item.title: Many states count mail ballots that arrive after Election Day. Those grace periods could go away
item.description: There will be just one Election Day for this fall’s midterm elections — Nov.

item.title: ‘We need to believe’: Matildas embrace underdogs tag for last shot at home glory in Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: The end is nigh, and Ellie Carpenter – even though she is not 26 for another month – knows it.

item.title: What’s the best solution to the world’s current problems? | Fiona Katauskas
item.description: Implementing it may be a challenge, howeverSee more of Fiona Katauskas’s cartoons here Continue reading.

item.title: South Australian election: Labor heads for a win, the Liberals a battering and One Nation a test of surging support
item.description: In an odd campaign Peter Malinauskas mocks Pauline Hanson’s anti-immigration stance and she finger-wags back.

item.title: The best bike lights in the US to see and be seen
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Matisse, 1941-1954 review – hit after glorious hit in a show of life-enhancing genius
item.description: Forget the joy and energy of youth – your best days might yet be ahead.

item.title: Cocktail of the week: Bear by Carlo Scotto’s wild garlic martini – recipe | The good mixer
item.description: It’s wild garlic season, which is as much cause for celebration on the drinks trolley as it is in the kitchen.

item.title: Add to playlist: the sharply observed electro-twee of the Femcels and the week’s best new tracks
item.description: The Femcels’ music is euphoric and depressive, sometimes ironic but mostly sincere, often high-tempo, and all delivered with wired, unvarnished vocals.

item.title: Greg James completes Comic Relief challenge, raising more than £4m
item.description: BBC Radio 1's Greg James has crossed the finish line of his Comic Relief tandem cycling challenge, raising more than £4m for charity.

item.title: ‘Weirdness, paranoia and extremity’: why HBO’s Neighbors is TV’s most fascinating show
item.description: Once upon a time, I worked as a local reporter in small-town Montana.

item.title: Live: Bulldog Baker's brilliant goal seals thrilling win over fast-finishing Crows
item.description: The Western Bulldogs have hung on to claim a thrilling six-point win over Adelaide, with a brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinching the win as the Crows stormed home.

item.title: Giddey sets new Aussie NBA record as Bulls fall short of miracle comeback
item.description: Josh Giddey has recorded a career-high 19 assists in a loss to Cleveland, the most by an Australian in a single NBA game.

item.title: Brisbane pull off grand final repeat in rematch with Storm
item.description: Brisbane have inflicted another second-half nightmare on the Storm, roaring back to win their grand final replay 18-14 in Melbourne to secure their first points of the NRL season.

item.title: More than 1,600 new gas wells approved for rural Queensland until 2081
item.description: The federal government has approved more than 1,600 new gas wells for rural Queensland until 2081.

item.title: Social supermarket praise for Comic Relief support
item.description: The former teaching assistant and her husband found themselves out of work because of ill health and were on benefits.

item.title: Helen Goh’s recipe for peanut and blackcurrant thumbprint cookies | The sweet spot
item.description: Niki Segnit writes in The Flavour Thesaurus that, while grape jelly is the familiar partner to peanut butter in the classic PBJ, she thinks blackcurrant, with its sharper, more complex character, would be a far better match for the fatty and salty peanuts.

item.title: Adorable alert! A Tasmanian possum has fallen asleep among the soft toys in an airport gift shop | First Dog on the Moon
item.description: The little guy just needed somewhere to napSign up here to get an email whenever First Dog cartoons are publishedGet all your needs met at the First Dog shop if what you need is First Dog merchandise and prints Continue reading.

item.title: 'Asking why honey is better than s***': Japan coach spins favouritism ahead of Matildas clash
item.description: It's an unusual question, posed by one of the most fascinating coaches at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: A bust of Barbra Streisand and beautiful memories: Richard E Grant’s garden – in seven extraordinary items
item.description: Step into Richard E Grant’s garden in Richmond, London, and you’ll be met with a rather unconventional sight.

item.title: Luka Dončić erupts for 60 as surging LA Lakers extend win streak in Miami
item.description: Luka Dončić scored 60 points, LeBron James had a triple-double on a night where he tied the NBA record for games played, and the Los Angeles Lakers pushed their season-best winning streak to eight games with a 134-126 win over the Miami Heat on Thursday night.

item.title: Teen happiness falls in western countries as social media hours rise
item.description: Costa Rica breaks into the World Happiness Report's top five, while Nordic countries stay on top and Australia stays outside the top 10.

item.title: Australia dig themselves out of a hole in women’s T20 win over West Indies
item.description: Australia got the Sophie Molineux era under way in sloppy style as a new-look side overcame six spilled chances before turning the screw late for a 43-run victory over West Indies in the first women’s T20 international in St Vincent.

item.title: Teen becomes youngest player since Nadal to win ATP Masters 1000 match
item.description: French tennis player Moise Kouame has become the youngest player to win a match at the Miami Open.

item.title: 'The ABC got lucky': How the VFA became must-watch TV for footy fans
item.description: Phil Cleary, Sam Kekovich, Peter Gee, Len Thompson and Ross Booth together in a promo shot for the ABC's VFA coverage.

item.title: Traitors, Amandaland - and Idris Elba - get set for Comic Relief
item.description: Marcella star Anna Friel is to play Claudia Winkleman in a sketch for this year's Comic Relief, alongside some recent stars of The Traitors such as Rachel Duffy and Faraaz Noor.

item.title: UK's most popular tourist attraction for 2025 takes top spot from British Museum
item.description: The Natural History Museum was the UK's most popular tourist attraction in 2025, breaking the record for the most visitors to any museum or gallery in a single year.

item.title: Natural History Museum tops UK attraction list with record visitors
item.description: London’s Natural History Museum (NHM) was the most popular attraction in the UK during 2025, with its renovated gardens, new climate gallery and lack of entry fee leading to record-breaking numbers of visitors.

item.title: High Point’s upset of Wisconsin ends the hope of a perfect March Madness bracket for millions

item.description: The war in the Middle East is escalating with more attacks on critical energy infrastructure.

item.title: Huge queues for jabs as meningitis vaccination scheme expanded
item.description: A meningitis vaccination programme has been expanded following a deadly outbreak in Kent - with university students queuing for hours for jabs.

item.title: Afroman keeps trolling cops after winning “Lemon Pound Cake” defamation case
item.description: Cops asked the jury for millions after Afroman used raid footage in music videos.

item.title: Premier League set to amend spending rules to give clubs more flexibility for transfers
item.description: The Premier League is poised to amend its new spending rules before they are introduced next season to give clubs greater flexibility in the transfer market.

item.title: ‘My taste is superb. My eyes are exquisite’: Dianne Wiest’s 20 best film performances – ranked!

item.description: It’s just after 11.30pm on a Friday in early March, and the air at Lux Frágil is already thick with excitement.

item.title: Show us your dome! Hot, balding, confident men are bringing sexy back | Rebecca Shaw
item.description: The Oscars were this week, and as usual I took the day off to judge the red carpet looks from my tracksuit pants-ed position on the couch, pick apart the monologue (love you Conan), declare I could write better presenter segments, and keep an eye on social media to see which stars cause the most buzz.

item.title: Striking gambit: Erling Haaland invests in new world chess championship
item.description: Erling Haaland has become a significant investor in a new world chess championship tour that is expected to star his fellow Norwegian Magnus Carlsen, the Guardian can reveal.

item.title: RFK Jr. makes food sound like a miracle drug. Researchers say he often overstates the science
item.description: In the Trump administration’s campaign to promote healthy eating, Robert F.

item.title: The housing charity helping key workers stay local
item.description: A housing organisation that offers key workers affordable rental properties has celebrated 20 years since it was founded.

item.title: Underscores: U review – ultra-imaginative auteur has pop’s most brilliant brain
item.description: April Grey is a US bedroom producer beloved of an impressive range of other artists – experimental pop duo 100 Gecs are fans, so is rapper Danny Brown and Blink-182 drummer Travis Barker – but thus far it’s been hard to accurately pin her down.

item.title: Nightclub linked to outbreak 'won't open until people are safe' says owner
item.description: The owner of a nightclub linked to the centre of a deadly meningitis outbreak in Kent says it "will not open until this is under control" after two members of staff were hospitalised.

item.title: Kick your tiredness with these 7 natural energy boosters
item.description: Feeling like a wilted flower most days? Perk up with these natural energy boosters.

item.title: César Chavez and Dolores Huerta led a movement that won better wages and conditions for farmworkers
item.description: Dolores Huerta and the late César Chavez are both labor rights icons credited with leading a movement that pushed growers to negotiate for better wages and working conditions for farmworkers.

item.title: Former DHS official on how the agency's next leader can be successful in the role
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former DHS official Miles Taylor about the confirmation hearing for Sen.

item.title: Mum 'so proud' of deafblind son's Eastenders debut
item.description: A mum whose five-year-old deafblind son has made his acting debut in Eastenders has said she is "so proud" of him and she hopes it will "break down barriers" for disabled children.

item.title: Live: Great Gunston gets Hawks home at the MCG
item.description: Hawthorn finishes over the top of an understrength Sydney side to knock off the ladder-leaders at the MCG, thanks to a four-goal haul from Jack Gunston.

item.title: Better NHS care might have saved 58 babies, BBC finds
item.description: At least 58 babies at an NHS maternity unit might have survived with better care, a BBC investigation has found.

item.description: Night buses will run to every borough in Greater Manchester as the city region expands its publicly controlled Bee Network.

item.description: It could easily have been a fluke.

item.description: Humour and science fiction may not seem obvious bedfellows but a history of cinema will tell you different.

item.title: Live: EU president to visit Australia as sides inch closer to signing free trade deal
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen will visit Australia next week to meet with the prime minister as Australia inches closer to signing a free trade agreement with Europe.

item.title: UConn teammates Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd headline AP All-America first team
item.description: Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd helped UConn to an undefeated season heading into March Madness.
